metadata.name: Hedron Archive — sacrifice-draw ability (ability_index: 0, explicit priority passes)
metadata.description: p1 has Hedron Archive untapped on the battlefield with {2} colorless mana already in the pool and two cards (Forest, Plains) on top of their library. p1 activates Hedron Archive's sacrifice-draw ability (ability_index: 0): pays {2}, taps Hedron Archive, and sacrifices it as part of the activation cost. The ability is placed on the stack while Hedron Archive is immediately in the graveyard (CR 602.2b + 601.2h: sacrifice is a cost paid before resolution). p1 passes priority, then p2 passes priority. The ability resolves, causing p1 to draw two cards (Forest and Plains). Final state: Hedron Archive in p1's graveyard, Forest and Plains in p1's hand, stack empty. This script complements script_stack_031 by explicitly using ability_index: 0 to identify the non-mana activated ability, and uses explicit sequential priority_pass actions (as in script_stack_054 for Mind Stone) rather than a single priority_round.
metadata.generation_notes: Hedron Archive oracle text (verified via MCP): '{T}: Add {C}{C}.' (mana ability — filtered out by enrich_spec_from_def) and '{2}, {T}, Sacrifice this artifact: Draw two cards.' The draw ability is the only non-mana activated ability, so ability_index: 0. CR 602.2a: the ability is announced and placed on the stack as a non-card object. CR 602.2b + 601.2h: costs are paid — {2} generic mana, tap Hedron Archive, sacrifice Hedron Archive. Sacrifice is paid as a cost (CR 601.2h), so Hedron Archive leaves the battlefield during cost payment, before the ability resolves; it enters the graveyard immediately. CR 608.2: the ability has no targets, so target-legality check is trivially satisfied. CR 608.2n: upon resolution the ability ceases to exist and is removed from the stack (abilities do not go to a graveyard on resolution). Library contains exactly two cards (Forest, Plains) so p1's two draws are deterministic and hand goes from 0 to 2 cards. Gotcha: must have 2 cards in library or DrawCards is silently a no-op (gotchas-infra.md). The {2} cost is paid as generic (colorless: 2 in mana_paid). Both priority passes are explicit (priority_pass for p1 then p2) to test that the engine requires each player to pass before resolving the top of stack.

script.step_note: p1 activates Hedron Archive's sacrifice-draw ability, both players pass explicitly, ability resolves drawing 2 cards.

actions.description: Initial: Hedron Archive on p1's battlefield (untapped), 2 cards in p1's library, stack empty

actions.note: CR 602.2a: p1 announces activation of Hedron Archive's draw ability (ability_index: 0 — only non-mana activated ability). The ability is placed on the stack. CR 602.2b + 601.2h: p1 pays the cost — {2} generic mana from pool, taps Hedron Archive, and sacrifices Hedron Archive. Hedron Archive moves to p1's graveyard immediately as part of cost payment.

actions.description: After activation: Hedron Archive in graveyard (sacrifice as cost), ability on stack, library still has 2 cards
actions.note: Sacrifice is a cost (CR 601.2h). Hedron Archive leaves the battlefield before the ability resolves — it is already in the graveyard at this point.
CR 116.3a: p1 passes priority after activating the ability.
CR 116.3: p2 passes priority. All players have passed in succession — top of stack resolves.

resolution.note: CR 120.2: p1 draws two cards (Forest then Plains) from the top of their library.

actions.note: CR 608.2: Ability resolves; no targets to check. p1 draws 2 cards. CR 608.2n: the ability is removed from the stack and ceases to exist (abilities do not go to a graveyard on resolution).

actions.description: Final: stack empty, p1 has 2 cards in hand, Hedron Archive in p1's graveyard, library empty
actions.note: Forest and Plains were the only cards in p1's library; both are now in hand. Hedron Archive sacrificed to graveyard as activation cost. Ability ceased to exist on resolution (CR 608.2n).
